title: Asset-registry v1.4 CORE build (Herald P1 Librarian-filing-loop dependency) per Delta Force PROCEED-WITH-CHANGES
summary: Operator directive: focus on completing the Marketing module; this is its Library-side dependency. title: Asset-registry fsync-fix — Windows os.fsync EBADF in _move_asset (unblocks the FAILED v1.4 CORE)
summary: Director APPROVED (channel, this ts): fix the Windows os.fsync EBADF in _move_asset that Grok's Windows re-run correctly caught (FAILED 7e7e53c, superseding false-PASS 3b87e43). HARD REQUIREMENT: executes-clean MUST RUN ON WINDOWS — no Linux-only evidence_ref (that was the false-PASS trap). Route through Library's coding gate per framework/22: builder != certifier -> executes-clean on Windows -> independent Regime-B Auditor -> Grok /cross-check re-cert on a real Windows re-run -> dual-PASS. Code on PR #70 branch fix/asset-registry-fsync-windows-ebadf; cert-coordination artifacts (this log entry + the grok-audit handoff) on MAIN so Hermes' poll discovers status:pending. Human-at-merge; Director merges #70 on dual-PASS.
acceptance: fsync runs on a writable fd (crash-safe temp->fsync->replace ordering per s9.3 preserved); tests.test_asset_registry green on Windows (was 20E+3F); full suite green on Windows; executes-clean evidence committed to tasks/evidence/; Regime-B Auditor PASS (independent Windows re-run, builder != auditor); Grok Windows PASS.
